sections.paragraph: (for שֵׂיֶה or שֵׂהֶה [“for שֵׂאֶה”]) const. שֵׂה with suff. שֵׂיֹו Deu 22:1, and שְׂיֵהוּ 1Sa 14:34 (both from שֵׂיֶה). Arab. شَاةُ and شَاهَةُ comm. a sheep or goat, a noun of unity, to which answers the collect. צאֹן (which see) a flock of sheep or. goats, Gen 22:7, Gen 22:8 30:32 Exo 12:3, seq. Where the particular species are to be distinguished more accurately, it is said, Deu 14:4, שֵׂה כְשָׂבִים וְשֵׂה עִזִּים “one of the flock of sheep and one of the flock of goats,” i.e. a sheep, a goat. Compare שֹׁור and בָּקָד.
